steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: For a system to have no solution, the two lines must be parallel: same slope, different y-intercepts. Line h (from the graph) has slope −10 and y-intercept −8, giving the equation 10x + y = −8. Line k is sx + 40y = t. For k to be parallel to h, the coefficient ratio must match: s/40 must equal 10/1, so s = 400. With s = 400, line k becomes 10x + y = t/40. The system has no solution as long as t/40 ≠ −8, i.e., t ≠ −320. The one value that is NOT possible is t = −320 (which makes the lines identical, giving infinitely many solutions instead).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '200': t/40 = 5 ≠ −8, so the lines are distinct and parallel — no solution exists. Possible. ✗
  • Choice B — '64': t/40 = 1.6 ≠ −8 — lines are parallel and distinct. Possible. ✗
  • Choice C — '−8': t/40 = −0.2 ≠ −8 — lines are parallel and distinct. Possible. ✗
  • Choice D — '−320': t/40 = −8, which is the same right-hand side as line h. The two equations are identical — the system has infinitely many solutions, not no solution. This value is NOT possible. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
